front: Film: a baby carriage rolls down the Odessa Steps as Cossack soldiers massacre civilians; a nurse with pince-nez glasses is shot and bleeds from her eye
Film: three separate shots of stone lion statues are edited together to give the illusion that the lions rise up in response to the massacre; a legless man crawls alongside the retreating crowd
Film: the Odessa Steps sequence was parodied in Terry Gilliam's Brazil (baby carriage replaced by a vacuum cleaner) and directly homaged in De Palma's The Untouchables (Chicago Union Station)
Film: the sequence begins after 'suddenly...' interrupts a shot of the waving crowd; a woman carries her trampled son back toward the soldiers

Film: first act 'Men and Maggots' — a mutiny triggered by maggot-infested borscht; a plate reading 'Give us this day our daily bread' is smashed; sailor leader Vakulinchuk dies and is laid in a tent on the pier

Film: a wild-haired priest bounces his cross into his palm while doctor Smirnov is killed; soldiers covering sailors under a tarpaulin are convinced not to shoot; the only color in the silent film is on a red flag
Film: directed by Sergei Eisenstein with cinematography by Eduard Tisse; commissioned after the success of his earlier film Strike (1924); the Berlin cut introduced Edmund Meisel's score
Film: the screaming nurse image directly inspired Francis Bacon's 'Study for the Nurse in the Battleship Potemkin, 1957' and his broader screaming popes series
Film: the final shot shows the battleship charging at the viewer appearing to split the screen; Chris Marker's A Grin Without a Cat opens with footage from this film

Film: Radu Jude made a short (The Potemkinists) about a sculptor making an inoffensive canal-side monument to the film's protagonists, and another 2022 film freeze-framing the final scene of men waving their hats
